'''Gate Master''' was a title used by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].

==Overview==
One of three [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] ranks within the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] [[Temple Security Force|Security Force]], it was bestowed upon the [[Jedi Peacekeeper]] tasked with guarding the four main entrances of the Temple on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. These entrances included the main Promenade entrance, the public entrance, the administrative entrance, and the south entrance. The Gate Master oversaw the appointment of [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] guards to oversee the entrances by day, and [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] from the Security Force to watch by night.<ref name="TJP" />

As of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Gate Master [[Jurokk]] had held the position for a considerable length of time. Remaining at the Temple throughout the war, Jurokk as well as [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] member [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Shaak Ti/Legends|Shaak Ti]] were placed in charge of securing the Temple as Master [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] set out to [[Showdown on Coruscant|arrest]] the [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] for being a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]]. While awaiting Windu's return, Jurokk was instead greeted at the main entrance by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]]. Looking out over the occupied [[Processional Way/Legends|Processional Way]], Jurokk realized too late that Skywalker had fallen to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. Stabbed through the head by Skywalker's [[Anakin Skywalker's second lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], Jurokk died before he could sound the alarm to warn the rest of the Temple.<ref name="ROTSn">[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'' novelization]]</ref>
